content: We can frame the intrinsic reward signal of curiosity-driven RL methods like ICM and RND as an active learning acquisition function that prioritizes storing only high-uncertainty novel transitions into an agent’s episodic memory, rather than caching every encountered state. This creates a self-pruning memory system that automatically allocates limited storage capacity to the high-information experiences that most improve the agent’s generalization and long-term planning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
